📌 Always use the template downloaded from the system — it contains the exact column structure SAP expects. Using a custom or outdated template will cause upload errors.
3
Fill in the Template
Open the template in Excel. It has two sections per journal entry:

Header section: BUKRS (Company Code), BLART (Document Type), BLDAT (Document Date), BUDAT (Posting Date), BKTXT (Header Text), WAERS (Currency)

Line Items section: BUKRS, HKONT (G/L Account), SGTXT (Item Text), WRSOL (Debit amount), WRHAB (Credit amount)

Fill in one header row and at least two line item rows per journal entry. Debits = WRSOL, Credits = WRHAB. Save the file.
⚠️ Every journal entry must balance: Total WRSOL must equal Total WRHAB. SAP will reject unbalanced entries during the upload validation.
4
Upload & Post
Back in the Fiori app, click Browse → select your completed Excel file → it appears in the upload list. Click Post. SAP processes all entries in the file and assigns document numbers. Click Show Log to see the result — successful postings show a green status with the document number; errors show a red status with the reason for failure.
✅ Use Show Log after every upload to confirm all entries posted correctly. If errors occurred, fix the template and re-upload only the failed entries.
✅ Result: Multiple journal entries uploaded and posted in one batch via Fiori. No need to enter each one individually in FB01 or F-02. Document numbers assigned for all successful entries. Full audit trail maintained in SAP.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Debits and credits not balancing for any entry in the template — SAP rejects any entry where Total Dr ≠ Total Cr.
✕Using incorrect column headers — the template columns must match exactly; do not rename or reorder any column.
✕Not checking Show Log after upload — some entries may have failed silently while others posted successfully.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB50Manual single-entry alternative for low posting volumes
FB03Verify uploaded documents individually after the batch posts
FBL3NReview all postings to confirm the batch upload is complete
SU01

User Maintenance — Add a Role

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Add the role SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT to user FI.MAMA. This will give the user access to all GL Accountant functions in SAP. Use SU01 to open the user record and assign the role.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Creates and maintains SAP user accounts — controls login credentials, authorization roles, user type, personal settings, and validity dates. Used by system administrators and security teams.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

A new FI accountant joins the company. The SAP Basis admin opens SU01 to create their user account, assigns the FI Accountant role with the correct authorization profile, and sets a temporary initial password.

1
Open SU01 — Initial Screen
Type SU01 in the command field. The User Maintenance: Initial Screen opens with a User field. Type FI.MAMA in the User field. The Alias field auto-fills with the user's full name (MOHAMED AHMED MAHMOUD ABDELGAWAD). Click the Edit (pencil) icon in the toolbar to open the user record for editing.
📌 SU01 is typically used by the SAP Basis team or security administrators. FI consultants use it to define which roles each user needs — based on the Authorization Matrix defined in the Blueprint.
2
Review Address Tab
The user record opens on the Address tab by default. You can see the user's personal details: Last Name, First Name, Full Name, Language, Department, Function. Verify this is the correct user before making any changes.
3
Navigate to Roles Tab & Add Role
Click the Roles tab. The Role Assignments section shows all roles currently assigned to this user. The existing roles are shown with green indicators and start/end dates. To add a new role: click in the empty Role field at the bottom of the list → type SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT → press Enter or use the search icon to find it.
💡 Role naming convention: SAP_BR_ = SAP Best Practice Business Role. Each role gives access to a specific set of T-codes and functions. Always set an appropriate end date (use 12/31/9999 for no expiry).
4
Save the User Record
After adding the role, click Save (bottom right). SAP saves the user record with the new role assignment. The user FI.MAMA can now access all GL Accountant functions in the system. The role will be active from the Start Date you set until the End Date.
✅ The change takes effect immediately after saving. The user does not need to log out and back in — the new role is active on their next transaction.
✅ Result: Role SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT successfully assigned to user FI.MAMA. The user now has GL Accountant access permissions in SAP. Role visible in the Roles tab with start/end dates.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Assigning too many roles (over-authorization) — users should only have access to the transactions their job requires.
✕Forgetting to set end dates for temporary users such as contractors or interns — accounts remain active indefinitely.
✕Not locking the account immediately when an employee leaves — this is a critical security risk.
🔗 Related T-Codes
SU53Authorization check — shows exactly why a user received an authorization error
FB03Verify which documents a user has posted after account setup
FBL3NFilter by User Name to see all postings made by a specific account
FS00

Create G/L Account Master Data

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Create a new P&L Revenue GL account 480002 called "Credit Revenue" in Company Code BEV1. You will configure the Type/Description tab, Control Data tab, and Create/Bank/Interest tab, then Save.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Creates and maintains GL account master data — the definition of each account: its type, account group, currency, tax settings, and controlling integration. An account must exist here before any postings can be made to it.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

The finance team decides to track a new cost category: "Digital Marketing Expenses." The consultant creates GL account 501450 in FS00 — after saving, the team can immediately start posting to it in FB50.

1
Open FS00 — Initial Screen
Enter T-Code FS00 in the SAP command field and press Enter. The "Edit G/L Account Centrally" screen opens with a left-panel chart of accounts tree and empty fields on the right. The G/L Account and Company Code fields are empty and ready for input.

Click the Create icon (blank page 📄) in the toolbar — a Create G/L Account popup appears with two empty fields: G/L Account and Company Code.
📌 FS00 opens in Edit mode by default. Use the Create icon in the toolbar to start a new account — do not type the account number in the top field before clicking Create.
2
Enter Account Number & Company Code
A Create G/L Account popup appears. Enter:

• G/L Account: 480002
• Company Code: BEV1

Click the green checkmark ✔ to confirm. SAP opens the full account creation screen.
💡 Account 480002 falls in the 400000–499999 Revenue range in this Chart of Accounts.
3
Tab 1: Type/Description — Empty Form
The Type/Description tab is now active and all fields are empty. Notice on the left panel: the P&L Statement Accounts folder is expanded, showing existing accounts (480001 Cash Revenue visible). The G/L Account Type and Account Group dropdowns are required fields.
4
Tab 1: Type/Description — Fill in Fields
Fill in all required fields on the Type/Description tab:

• G/L Account Type: P — Primary Costs or Revenue
• Account Group: PL — P&L Statement Accounts
• Short Text: Credit Revenue
• G/L Acct Long Text: Credit Revenue
💡 Account Type "P" creates a P&L account linked to CO (Controlling). This requires a Cost Element Category setting later on the Control Data tab.
5
Tab 2: Control Data — Account Control Section
Click the Control Data tab. Fill in the top section:

• Account Currency: EGP (Egyptian Pound)
• Tax Category: * (All tax types allowed)
• Posting without tax allowed: ✓ Checked

Leave Alternative Account No. and Tolerance Group empty.
6
Tab 2: Control Data — CO Settings → Save
Scroll down on the Control Data tab to see Account Settings in Controlling Area. Fill in:

• CElem Category: 11 — Revenue

Leave Record Quantity and Internal UoM unchecked/empty. Then click Save (bottom right). SAP creates account 480002 and it appears in the left-panel tree under P&L Statement Accounts.
⚠️ CElem Category is mandatory for Account Type "P". 1 = Primary Costs, 11 = Revenue, 12 = Sales Deduction. After saving, verify the account appears alongside 480001 in the left panel.
7
Tab 3: Create/Bank/Interest — Final Save
Click the Create/bank/interest tab. Set the Field Status Group (e.g. G001 — General). Then click Save. SAP creates account 480002 "Credit Revenue" and it now appears in the left-panel account tree under P&L Statement Accounts.
✅ After saving, you can verify by navigating to the account in the left panel — it appears under P&L Statement Accounts alongside 480001.
✅ Result: GL Account 480002 "Credit Revenue" is now created in Company Code BEV1 — Type P (Revenue), Currency EGP, CElem 11. It is ready for journal entry postings via FB50 or F-02.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Wrong Account Group — this controls which fields are required and what the account can be used for; it cannot be changed after creation.
✕Forgetting to set CElem Category for P-type (P&L) accounts — postings will fail with a CO error if this is missing.
✕Creating only at Chart of Accounts level and forgetting the Company Code extension — both levels are required before posting.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB50Post to the newly created account immediately after saving
FBL3NView all transactions posted to this GL account
FAGLB03View the period balance of the account after postings
FB50

Post G/L Document — Modern Interface

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Post a Buffet Expense journal entry of EGP 1,000 — debit account 501303 (Buffet EXP) and credit account 122223 (CIB Bank). Use Document Date 02/05/2026 and Reference "TEST".
🔍 What is this T-Code?

The modern Fiori-style interface for posting G/L journal entries. Supports multiple line items in a spreadsheet-like grid with document simulation and balance verification before final posting.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

The accountant records monthly utility bills — Dr: Electricity Expense 5,000 / Dr: Water Expense 1,200 / Cr: Accrued Liabilities 6,200. FB50's clean grid layout makes entering multiple line items fast.

1
Open FB50 — Empty Screen
Enter T-Code FB50 and press Enter. The "Enter G/L Account Document" screen opens. Note the two main areas:

• Top half (Header): Document Date, Currency, Reference, Doc Header Text
• Bottom half (Line Items): G/L account grid with D/C selector and Amount fields

The line item grid shows 5 empty rows ready for input. Total Dr and Total Cr show 0.
2
Fill in the Document Header
Fill in the header fields at the top of the screen:

• Document Date: 02/05/2026
• Currency: EGP (auto-filled)
• Reference: TEST
• Doc. Header Text: Test

Posting Date auto-fills to today. Company Code BEV1 is shown at the bottom of the header area.
💡 The green indicator (●●■) in the Amount Information box changes as you add line items — it shows document balance status.
3
Enter Line Items — Debit & Credit
In the line items grid, enter both journal entry lines:

Line 1 (Debit):
• G/L Acct: 501303 → Short Text auto-fills: "Buffet EXP"
• D/C: S Debit
• Amount: 1,000

Line 2 (Credit):
• G/L Acct: 122223 → Short Text auto-fills: "CIB Bank"
• D/C: H Credit
• Amount: 1,000

The Amount Information box now shows Total Dr: 1,000 = Total Cr: 1,000 ✅
⚠️ SAP uses German notation internally: S = Soll (Debit), H = Haben (Credit). This appears in the D/C dropdown and in posted document displays.
4
Tax Check Screen & Post
When you click Post, SAP may show the "Enter Accounting Document: Check Tax Data" screen. This appears because the GL accounts have tax categories configured. The screen shows:

• Tax code 29 (PstKy 40) — Tax Base Amount: 1,000
• Tax code A0 (PstKy 50) — Tax Base Amount: 1,000
• Warning: "Tax entered incorrect (code 29, amount 0), correct 140 EGP"

Since we are not applying tax in this exercise, confirm and post through this screen.
💡 The "Calculate Tax" checkbox at the top controls whether SAP calculates tax automatically. Leave it unchecked for this exercise.
✅ Result: Journal entry posted — Buffet EXP (501303) Dr 1,000 / CIB Bank (122223) Cr 1,000. SAP assigns a permanent Document Number. Use FB03 to display and verify the posted document.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Document not balanced (Total Dr ≠ Total Cr) — SAP will not allow posting until both sides match exactly.
✕Forgetting to assign a Cost Center for P&L expense accounts — these accounts require a CO cost object.
✕Wrong Document Date vs Posting Date — Document Date is the original invoice/transaction date; Posting Date determines the accounting period.
🔗 Related T-Codes
F-02Legacy alternative for complex entries requiring posting keys or special GL indicators
FB03Verify the posted document immediately after
FB08Reverse the entry if a mistake is discovered after posting
F-02

Classic G/L Posting — Legacy Interface

Advanced
📋 Scenario
Post a Cleaning Expense of EGP 1,000 using F-02 — the classic SAP interface. Debit account 500005 (Cleaning Exp) with PstKy 40, and Credit account 122225 (BANK) with PstKy 50. Then verify the posted document using FB03.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

The classic legacy screen interface for complex GL postings. Uses explicit posting keys (40=Debit, 50=Credit) and supports special GL indicators. Still widely used in training and for specific transaction types.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

An experienced consultant uses F-02 in training because it exposes the full posting engine — posting keys, document types, and special GL indicators — that FB50 hides behind its simplified modern interface.

1
Open F-02 — Header Screen (Empty)
Enter T-Code F-02 and press Enter. The "Enter G/L Account Posting: Header Data" screen appears with some fields pre-filled:

• Posting Date: auto-fills to today (05/02/2026)
• Type: SA (General G/L Document) — default
• Company Code: BEV1 — pre-filled from user settings
• Currency: EGP

Document Date and Reference are empty — you must fill them.
💡 F-02 uses a 2-step process: first fill the Header, then enter each Line Item on a separate screen — unlike FB50 where everything is on one screen.
2
Fill in the Document Header — Press Enter
Fill in the Header fields then press Enter:

• Document Date: 2/5/2026
• Type: SA — General G/L Document (pre-filled)
• Company Code: BEV1 (pre-filled)
• Posting Date: 05/02/2026 (pre-filled)
• Period: 5 — auto-calculated by SAP
• Currency: EGP
• Reference: TEST
• Doc. Header Text: Test

After pressing Enter, SAP navigates to the first Line Item input screen.
3
Enter Line Items One by One
This is the key difference in F-02 — each line item is entered on a separate screen using Posting Keys:

Line 1 (Debit):
• PstKy: 40 → Enter → Account: 500005 (Cleanning exp) → Enter
• Amount: 1,000 EGP
• SAP shows: "Item 1 / Debit entry / 40"
• Profit Center ASSPC02 auto-fills from account settings

Line 2 (Credit):
In the "Next Line Item" section at the bottom:
• PstKy: 50 → Account: 122225 (BANK) → Enter → Amount: 1,000

Status bar shows: "Specify account number" — SAP prompts for the next line's account.
💡 Posting Key 40 = GL Debit. Posting Key 50 = GL Credit. These are the two standard keys for GL-to-GL journal entries.
4
Simulate — Display Overview
After entering all line items, click Simulate from the top menu. The "Display Overview" screen appears showing a summary before posting:

• Document Number: INTERNAL — not yet saved, just a preview
• Items in document currency:
  — Item 001 | PK 40 | 500005 Cleanning exp | 1,000 Dr
  — Item 002 | PK 50 | 122225 BANK | 1,000- Cr
• Bottom: D 1,000 = C 1,000 ✅ — entry is balanced
• 2 Line Items confirmed

If everything looks correct → click Post.
✅ Always simulate before posting to catch errors. The overview screen is your final chance to verify the entry before it becomes permanent.
5
Post — Confirmation Message
After clicking Post, SAP returns to the empty Header screen and shows a confirmation message at the bottom of the screen:

✅ Document 100000047 was posted in company code BEV1

This means:
• The entry is permanently saved in SAP
• Document Number: 100000047
• It cannot be deleted — only reversed with a reversal document
• SAP is now ready for the next journal entry
6
Verify with FB03 — Display Document
Enter T-Code FB03 and enter Document Number 100000047 to display the posted document. The Data Entry View shows:

• Company Code: BEV1 | Fiscal Year: 2026 | Period: 5
• Document Date: 02/05/2026 | Posting Date: 05/02/2026
• Currency: EGP

Line Items:
• Item 1 | S 40 | 500005 | Cleanning exp | 1,000 Dr EGP
• Item 2 | H 50 | 122225 | BANK | 1,000 Cr EGP
💡 S = Soll (German for Debit) | H = Haben (German for Credit). SAP uses German accounting notation internally in posted document displays.
✅ Result: Document 100000047 posted — Cleaning Expenses (500005) Dr 1,000 / BANK (122225) Cr 1,000. Verified in FB03. The document is now permanent in SAP's audit trail.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Wrong Posting Key — 40 = Debit GL, 50 = Credit GL. Using the wrong key will reverse your debit/credit and corrupt the entry.
✕Confusing Document Date with Posting Date — a common error that places entries in the wrong accounting period.
✕Forgetting to Simulate before posting — always preview the document to catch errors before they become permanent.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB50Modern alternative for straightforward GL journal entries
FB03Verify the document number shown in the confirmation message
FB08Reverse the entry if an error is found after posting
FB03

Display & Analyze a Posted Document

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Use FB03 to find all documents posted by user FI.MAMA in Company Code BEV1. Open a document, switch to General Ledger View, change the layout, export to Excel, view the Document Header, and explore the related Accounting Documents via the Environment menu.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

A read-only transaction to view any posted FI document. Shows all line items, amounts, document header details, GL view, and links to all related sub-ledger documents.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

Your manager asks "who posted document 100000084 and what accounts were hit?" Open FB03, enter the document number, and see the full entry — user, timestamp, every debit and credit line — without changing anything.

🔍 What is this T-Code?

A SAP Fiori app for uploading and posting multiple journal entries in bulk using an Excel template — eliminating the need to post each entry individually in FB50 or F-02.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

Month-end closing requires 60 depreciation and accrual entries. The accountant fills the Excel template with all entries, uploads it via the Fiori app, and all 60 are posted in one action — saving several hours of manual work.

🔍 What is this T-Code?

Generates an Accounts Payable aging analysis — groups all outstanding vendor invoices by how many days overdue they are. Essential for cash flow planning and payment prioritization.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

End of month, the CFO asks: "Which vendors are we overdue with, and by how long?" IDCNAP produces the full AP aging report in seconds, showing every vendor with amounts distributed across overdue day buckets.

1
Open FB03 — Document List
Type FB03 in the SAP command field and press Enter. The initial screen opens — click the Document List button in the toolbar. The Document List selection screen appears with fields: Company Code, Document Number, Fiscal Year, Ledger, and General Selections (Document Type, Posting Date, Entry Date).
📌 Leave all fields empty for a broad search — or pre-fill Company Code BEV1 and Fiscal Year 2026. The more filters you apply, the faster results load on large systems.
2
Use Dynamic Selection to Filter by User
Click the Dynamic Selection button (left side of the toolbar). A panel expands on the left side showing additional filterable fields under Document header. Check the box next to User Name — this adds it to the right-side filter panel. Enter FI.MAMA in the User Name field, then click Execute.
💡 Dynamic Selection is the way to search by any field not shown on the standard screen — including User Name, Transaction Code, Changed On date, and more.
3
View Results & Open a Document
SAP returns the Document List showing all documents posted by FI.MAMA — in our example, 3 documents are displayed (100000084, 100000047, 100000015), all type SA (Financial Accounting), currency EGP. Double-click any document to open it. The Display Document: Data Entry View opens showing the document header and all line items.
📌 The columns shown are: CoCode, DocumentNo, Year, Type, Currency, Doc.Date, Posting Date, Entered On, User.
4
Switch to General Ledger View & Change Layout
From the Data Entry View, click General Ledger View in the toolbar. The document now shows GL account numbers instead of customer/vendor codes — useful for verifying the underlying accounting impact. To customize the columns, click Settings → Change Layout. The layout popup lets you add or remove columns (e.g. add Company Code, remove unused columns). Click the green checkmark to apply.
💡 S = Soll (Debit in German) | H = Haben (Credit in German) — SAP uses German notation for debit/credit indicators in document displays.
5
View Document Header & Export to Excel
To see full document metadata: go to Document → Display Document Header. The popup shows Document Type, Reference, Posting Date, Created By, Entry Time, T-Code used to post (FB01), and Logical System.

To export: click the Spreadsheet export icon in the toolbar → a Select Spreadsheet dialog appears → choose Excel (XLSX) format → click the green checkmark. SAP exports all line items to an Excel file.
📌 The Document Header is key for audit purposes — it tells you exactly who posted, when, and from which T-code.
6
Explore Document Environment — Accounting Documents
From the GL View, go to Environment → Document Environment → Accounting Documents. A popup appears listing all documents that SAP automatically created alongside the original entry. In the example, you can see: Accounting document (the main FI entry), Profit center document, Controlling document, and Profitability Analysis document — all generated automatically from one posting.
✅ This is very useful for understanding the full financial impact of a posting across FI, CO, and CO-PA modules — all linked to the same original document number.
✅ Result: Successfully searched documents by user via Dynamic Selection, opened a posted document, viewed GL accounts in GL View, customized the layout, exported to Excel, inspected the Document Header, and explored the full set of automatically generated accounting documents via Environment.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕FB03 is display-only — you cannot edit anything here. Many beginners look for a Save button that simply does not exist.
✕Forgetting to use Dynamic Selection to filter by User Name — it is not visible on the main screen by default.
✕Not switching to GL View — the default Data Entry View shows posting keys, not the actual GL account numbers.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB08Reverse the document if it was posted incorrectly
FBL3NView all line items for a specific GL account
FAGLB03View period-by-period balances for an account
FB08

Reverse a Posted Document

Beginner
📋 Scenario
A journal entry was posted with the wrong amount in Company Code BEV1, Fiscal Year 2026. Use FB08 to reverse it. You cannot delete it — SAP requires a reversal document instead.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Creates a mirror-image reversal document that cancels the accounting effect of a previously posted entry. SAP never deletes posted documents — a reversal is the only correction method.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

A colleague posted rent expense of 10,000 EGP to the wrong account. You cannot delete it — use FB08 to create a reversal that zeroes it out, then re-post the corrected entry with FB50.

1
Open FB08
Type FB08 in the command field and press Enter. The Reverse Document: Header Data screen opens with fields for Document Number, Company Code, Fiscal Year, Reversal Reason, Posting Date, and Posting Period.
⚠️ SAP rule: You can never delete a posted document. FB08 creates a mirror-image reversal entry that cancels the original — both remain permanently in the system for audit purposes.
2
Enter Document Details
Fill in:
• Document Number: the number of the wrong document (from FB03)
• Company Code: BEV1
• Fiscal Year: 2026

You can also click Document List in the toolbar to search and select the document from a list instead of typing the number manually.
💡 Use Display Before Reversal button first to confirm you have the right document before posting the reversal.
3
Enter Reversal Reason & Posting Date
Fill in the Specifications for Reverse Posting section:
• Reversal Reason: 01 (Reversal in current period — the most common reason)
• Posting Date: today's date or the date the reversal should be effective
• Posting Period: leave blank (SAP will auto-fill from the posting date)
📌 Reversal Reason 01 = reversal in the current period. Other codes exist for prior-period reversals or specific correction types.
4
Post the Reversal
Click Post. SAP creates a new reversal document — a mirror image of the original with all debits and credits swapped. SAP shows the new document number in the status bar at the bottom. Use FB03 to verify both documents (the original and the reversal) are now in the system.
✅ After reversal: the original document and the reversal document both remain in SAP permanently. The net accounting effect is zero — they cancel each other out.
✅ Result: The wrong document is reversed. A new reversal document is created with all amounts mirrored. The GL balances are corrected. Both documents remain in SAP's permanent audit trail.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Trying to reverse an already-reversed document — SAP will block this with an error message.
✕Using Reversal Reason 01 for a prior-period document — use the correct reason code for prior-period reversals.
✕Not verifying the document in FB03 first — always confirm you have the right document before posting a reversal.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB03Find and verify the document before reversing
FB50Re-post the corrected entry after the reversal
F-02Alternative posting interface to re-post the corrected entry
FBV0

Find & Complete a Parked Document

Beginner
📋 Scenario
A junior accountant parked a document in Company Code BEV1 for review. Use FBV0 to find it, review it, and submit it for approval by clicking Save as Completed.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Lists and manages parked (draft) documents that have NOT yet updated GL balances. These documents are pending review or approval before final posting.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

A junior accountant parks a month-end accrual for supervisor review. The supervisor opens FBV0, verifies the amounts and accounts, then clicks Save as Completed to send it through the approval workflow.

1
Open FBV0
Type FBV0 in the command field. The List of Parked Documents screen opens. Notice it has sections for: Company Code range, Document Number, Fiscal Year, General Selections (posting date, document date, document type, reference, entered by), and Processing Status.
💡 Parked documents have NOT updated GL balances yet. They are drafts awaiting review or approval before final posting.
2
Enter Search Filters & Execute
Enter:
• Company Code: BEV1
• Entered by: the user who parked the document (e.g. FI.MAMA)

In the Processing Status section, you can filter by: Enter Release, Complete, or Released. Leave blank to see all parked documents. Click Execute.
📌 Processing Status: blank = not yet completed. "Complete" = submitted for approval. "Released" = approved and ready to post.
3
Open the Parked Document
SAP shows the list of matching parked documents. Double-click on a document to open it. Review the line items, amounts, and accounts. You can edit any field while it is still in parked status — it behaves like a draft.
💡 Anyone with the correct authorization can open and edit a parked document — unlike a Held document, which only the original user can retrieve.
4
Save as Completed → Enters Approval Workflow
After reviewing the document, click Save as Completed (found in the Document menu or toolbar). The document is now flagged as Complete and enters the approval workflow. An authorized approver will see it in their workflow queue and can release it for final posting.
✅ Save as Completed ≠ Post. The document is NOT yet posted — it still needs to go through the approval chain before it updates GL balances.
✅ Result: The parked document was found, reviewed, and submitted into the approval workflow via Save as Completed. It now awaits release by an authorized approver before final posting.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Confusing Parked with Held documents — held docs can only be retrieved by the original user; parked docs are accessible to anyone with the right authorization.
✕Clicking Post instead of Save as Completed — this bypasses the approval workflow entirely.
✕Forgetting that parked documents have NO effect on GL balances until they are finally approved and posted.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB50Post directly without parking — for simple entries requiring no approval
FB03View the document after it is finally posted
FB08Reverse the document if an error is found after posting
FBL3N

Display GL Account Line Items

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Review all transactions posted to GL account 110010 (Petty Cash A/C) in Company Code BEV1. Display open items and analyze the individual postings using FBL3N.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Displays all individual debit and credit postings to any GL account. Can filter by open items, cleared items, or all items within a selected date range.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

An external auditor requests a full listing of every transaction posted to the Petty Cash account for the year. FBL3N produces the complete list — document numbers, dates, amounts — in seconds.

1
Open FBL3N — Selection Screen
Type FBL3N in the command field. The G/L Account Line Item Display screen opens. Enter:
• G/L account: 110010 (or any account you want to review)
• Company code: BEV1
• Under Line item selection — Status, select Open Items and set an Open at Key Date (e.g. today's date)
📌 You can also select "Cleared Items" (already matched/closed) or "All Items" to see the complete history of postings to the account.
2
Execute & Read the Results
Click Execute. SAP displays all line items for the selected account showing: Status (✓ = open), Assignment, Document Number, Business Area, Document Type, Document Date, Posting Key, Amount in Local Currency, Currency, Clearing Document, and Text. The total at the bottom shows the sum of all displayed items.
💡 Each row is one line item from one document. Click on any document number to drill into the original posted document in FB03.
3
Try FAGLL03 — Same Report, GL View
Now type FAGLL03 in the command field. You get the same line item display but from the General Ledger view — with additional columns like G/L Account, Ledger, and Ledger-specific amounts. The selection screen and layout are similar to FBL3N. FAGLL03 is the preferred T-code in S/4HANA environments.
📌 FBL3N = classic view (ECC). FAGLL03 = GL view (S/4HANA preferred). Both give you line items — FAGLL03 adds ledger-level detail.
✅ Result: All line items for GL account 110010 (Petty Cash) displayed. Each individual posting is visible with document number, date, type, and amount. Drilldown to original documents available. Same data viewed in both FBL3N and FAGLL03.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Forgetting to set the Open at Key Date when selecting Open Items — results may be incorrect or show no data.
✕Running with no date restriction on a busy account — can return thousands of lines and slow down the system.
✕Not customizing the layout to add useful columns like Reference, Header Text, Cost Center, or Clearing Date.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FAGLB03View period totals instead of individual line items
FB03Open any specific document directly from the results list
FBL1NSame concept but for vendor accounts instead of GL accounts
FAGLB03

Display GL Account Period Balances

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Review the monthly balance movement of GL account 122225 (BANK) in Company Code BEV1 for Fiscal Year 2026 using FAGLB03.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Displays GL account balances broken down by accounting period — showing debit total, credit total, net balance, and cumulative running balance for each month of the fiscal year.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

Month-end closing: the controller needs the closing balance of the bank account for each month to reconcile with the bank statement. FAGLB03 shows the period-by-period breakdown of account 122225 instantly.

1
Open FAGLB03 — Input Screen
Type FAGLB03 in the command field. The G/L Account Balance Display screen opens. Enter:
• Account Number: 122225
• Company Code: BEV1
• Fiscal Year: 2026
• Ledger: 0L (Leading Ledger — the main accounting ledger)
📌 Ledger 0L = the Leading Ledger in SAP. This is the standard ledger used for financial statements. Other ledgers may exist for parallel accounting (e.g. IFRS vs local GAAP).
2
Execute & Read the Balance Table
Click Execute. SAP displays the Balance Display: G/L Accounts For the Ledger 0L screen. The table shows one row per fiscal period (1–16) with columns: Period, Debit, Credit, Balance (net for that period), and Cumulative Balance (running total from period 1). The first row shows the Bal.Carryforward (opening balance from prior year).
💡 A negative balance shown without brackets means credit balance (normal for bank/asset accounts that are credit-heavy). Click on any period row to drill down to the individual line items for that period.
3
Analyze the Results
In the example for account 122225 (BANK): Periods 1–4 show a Credit of 200 each period, with the cumulative balance increasing to 800- by period 4. Period 5 shows a Debit of 72,583 and Credit of 965,911 — resulting in a large movement. Periods 6–12 show no new activity (balance carries forward). This view immediately tells you when account activity happened during the year.
✅ FAGLB03 is the go-to report for monthly account reconciliation — quickly spot which periods had unexpected activity or large movements.
✅ Result: Period-by-period balance for GL account 122225 (BANK) displayed. Debit, Credit, Balance, and Cumulative Balance shown for each fiscal period. Drill-down to line items available per period.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Not selecting the correct Ledger — always use 0L (Leading Ledger) unless your company uses parallel accounting.
✕Confusing FAGLB03 (period totals) with FBL3N (individual line items) — use FAGLB03 for summaries, FBL3N for detail.
✕Forgetting to specify the fiscal year — FAGLB03 shows one fiscal year at a time.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FBL3NDrill into individual line items for any specific period
FK10NSame period-balance concept but for vendor accounts
FB03Open any specific document from the drilldown
FBCJ

Cash Journal — View Cash Transactions

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Open the Cash Journal for Company Code BEV1 and review the Opening Balance, total cash movements, Closing Balance, and individual cash transactions for the current period.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Manages a petty cash fund — records cash receipts and payments directly with a running balance, and automatically generates the corresponding GL accounting document.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

The office manager pays 200 EGP for printer cartridges from the petty cash box. She records it in FBCJ as a cash payment, and SAP automatically creates the GL entry: Dr Stationery Expense / Cr Petty Cash.

1
Open FBCJ — Initial Popup
Type FBCJ in the command field. A Cash Journal: Initial popup appears asking for:
• Company Code: BEV1
• Cash Journal: the cash journal number (e.g. TEST for the training journal)

Enter both values and click the green checkmark ✔ to open the journal.
📌 A company can have multiple cash journals (e.g. one per branch or department). Each has its own journal number and is linked to a specific GL Petty Cash account.
2
Review the Balance Display
The Cash Journal main screen opens. On the right, the Balance Display for Display Period section shows:
• Opening Balance: cash on hand at the start of the period (e.g. 999,000 EGP)
• + Total Cash Receipts and + Total Check Receipts
• - Total Cash Payments
• = Closing Balance: current cash on hand
• Cash Thereof: physical cash amount

Use the shortcuts Today / This Week / Current Period to change the display range.
3
Browse Transaction Tabs
At the bottom, three tabs show individual transactions:
• Cash Payments — all outgoing cash entries (expenses paid in cash)
• Cash Receipts — all incoming cash entries (cash received)
• Check Receipts — checks received

Each tab shows: Business Transaction, Amount, Document Number, G/L Account, Text, Supplier/Customer, Posting Date. Use Print Cash Journal to generate a printable petty cash report.
✅ Every transaction posted in FBCJ immediately updates the corresponding GL account — no separate journal entry needed. SAP handles it automatically.
4
Post a Payment to Vendor
On the Cash Payments tab, click the Business Transaction field → a search popup appears listing all configured transactions (CASH SALE, PAYMENT TO VENDOR, PURCHASES USING CASH, etc.). Select PAYMENT TO VENDOR (type K, Tran. 0007).

Fill in: Amount 5,000 | G/L Acct auto-fills to 210100 | Receipt Recip: Company Behery | Supplier: 446031

Click Save Sel. → then Post Sel. → Balance updates: Total Cash Payments: 5,000 | Closing Balance: 44,900.
📌 After posting, use Follow-On Docs to see the generated accounting document: Petty Cash 2 (110011) Cr 5,000 / Behery (446031) Dr 5,000.
5
Reverse a Cash Transaction
To reverse a posted cash entry, select the transaction row (check the checkbox) → click the (−) Reverse button in the bottom toolbar (highlighted in red). A "Reverse document?" popup appears → click Yes.

A "Reason for reversal" dialog appears: enter Reversal reason 01 | Posting Date: today → confirm with ✔.

The journal now shows both the original and reversed entries. Closing balance returns to 49,900 (opening balance).
⚠️ You cannot simply delete a cash journal entry. Always reverse it — both entries remain in the system for audit trail purposes.
6
Post an Expense — Purchases Using Cash
For an expense payment (not vendor-related), select business transaction PURCHASES USING CASH (type E). Fill in: Amount 8,000 | G/L Account 500002 (Electricity EXP) | Cost Center INVCS01.

Save → Post → Document shows: Petty Cash 2 (110011) Cr 8,000 / Electricity EXP (500002) Dr 8,000. Total Cash Payments now: 8,000 | Closing Balance: 41,900.
💡 Use PURCHASES USING CASH for expense payments. Use PAYMENT TO VENDOR when paying a known vendor account (posts to reconciliation account 210100).
7
View All Available Business Transactions
Click the search icon in the Business Transaction field. The Restrict Value Range popup opens (8 Entries found for Company Code BEV1):

• CASH SALE (0010) — Type R, G/L 400000
• CUSTODIES FROM (0014) — Type K, Special G/L A
• PAYMENT FROM CUSTOMER (0008) — Type D
• PAYMENT TO VENDOR (0007) — Type K
• PURCHASES USING CASH (0009) — Type E, G/L 500000
• REVENUE (0012) — Type R, G/L 400000
• TRANSFER BANK TO JOURNAL (0005) — Type C, G/L 140100
• TRANSFER JOURNAL TO BANK (0006) — Type B, G/L 140100
📌 Business transactions are configured per company code. The list you see is specific to BEV1 — different companies may have different transactions available.
✅ Result: Cash Journal for BEV1 opened successfully. Opening and Closing Balances reviewed. Individual cash payment and receipt transactions visible. Ready to post new cash transactions or print the journal report.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Forgetting to click Post Selected after saving — FBCJ entries must be posted to update GL balances.
✕Posting to the wrong business transaction type — the type determines which GL account and document type SAP uses.
✕Finding an error after posting — use the (−) Reverse button inside FBCJ, not the external FB08 transaction.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB50For GL journal entries not involving the petty cash fund
FBL3NVerify the GL postings automatically created by FBCJ
FB03View the full details of the follow-on accounting document
BP

Create a New Vendor (Business Partner)

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Create a new vendor in SAP S/4HANA using transaction BP. The vendor is Mr. Gawad, a person-type supplier in Cairo, Egypt. Assign both the FI Supplier role (with reconciliation account 210100) and the Purchasing role (with Purchasing Org BE25).
🔍 What is this T-Code?

The unified Business Partner transaction in SAP S/4HANA for creating vendors, customers, and other partners. Replaces the legacy XK01 (vendor) and XD01 (customer) transactions used in ECC.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

A new supplier signs a contract. Before their first invoice can be processed, the AP team creates them in BP with the FI Vendor role (reconciliation account) and Purchasing role. Without this, FB60 will reject the entry.

1
Open BP — Maintain Business Partner
Type BP in the command field and press Enter. The Maintain Business Partner screen opens with two tabs: Worklist and Find. The toolbar shows buttons: Person, Organization, Group. Use the Find tab to search existing partners or click Person to create a new one.
💡 In SAP S/4HANA, customers and vendors are unified as Business Partners. One BP can have multiple roles (General, Supplier, Customer) assigned at the same time.
2
Create Person — Fill Basic Data
Click Person in the toolbar. The "Create Person" screen opens. Fill in:

• Grouping: AF VENDOR FOR PG
• Title: 0002 Mr.
• First name: Gawad
• Last name: Gawad

The Search Term 1/2 auto-fills to GAWAD / GAWAD.
📌 The Grouping determines the number range for the BP number. "AF VENDOR FOR PG" assigns the correct range for AP vendors in the system.
3
Fill Street Address
Scroll down on the Address tab to the Standard Address section. Fill in:

• Street/House number: Cairo
• Postal Code: 11111
• City: cairo
• Country/Region: EG (Egypt)
• Region: C → auto-fills to Cairo
• Time zone: EGYPT (auto-fills)

Press Enter → status bar shows "Data of business partner is error-free".
4
Save → Business Partner Created
Click Save. SAP creates the Business Partner and assigns a number (e.g. 446208). The screen switches to Display mode showing:

• Business Partner: 446208
• Name: Gawad Gawad / 11111 cairo
• Display in BP role: 000000 Business Partner (Gen.)
• Status bar: "Business partner 446208 created"
✅ The BP is now created as a General Business Partner only. The Vendor and Purchasing roles must be added in the next steps.
5
Add FI Supplier Role (FLVN00) — Company Code Data
In the "Change in BP role" dropdown (top of screen), select FLVN00 Supplier (Fin.Accounting). The screen title changes to "Change Person: 446208, new role Supplier (Fin.Accounting)".

Navigate to the Company Code section:
• Company Code: BEV1 (be7ery) — auto-populated
• Supplier number auto-assigned as <External>

Go to the Vendor: Account Management tab:
• Reconciliation acct: 210100 → description: "Vendors Reconciliation"

Press Enter → "Data of business partner is error-free" → Click Save.
The Supplier field now shows 446208 (same as BP number).
📌 The Reconciliation Account (210100) links the vendor sub-ledger to the GL. Every invoice or payment posted to vendor 446208 will automatically update GL account 210100.
6
Add Purchasing Role (FLVN01) — Purchasing Org Data
Switch the role to FLVN01 Supplier (New). The Purchasing tab becomes visible in the toolbar. Navigate to the Purchasing Organization section:

• Purch. organization: BE25 → description: "be7ery's po"

The Purchasing Data tab shows: Order currency, Payment Terms, Incoterms, etc. (leave blank for now).

Press Enter → "Data of business partner is error-free" → Click Save.
Status bar: "Changes have been saved". Role now shows as FLVN01 Supplier (Maintained).
✅ Vendor 446208 is now fully configured with General data, FI Accounting role (recon. acct 210100), and Purchasing role (Org BE25). Ready for invoice entry in FB60.
✅ Result: Business Partner 446208 (Mr. Gawad Gawad) created as a full vendor — General BP role, FI Supplier role with Recon. Account 210100 (Company Code BEV1), and Purchasing role with Purchasing Org BE25.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Not assigning both the FI Vendor role AND the Purchasing role — both are required for full AP invoice processing.
✕Wrong Reconciliation Account — this links the vendor sub-ledger to the GL; an incorrect account causes balance sheet errors.
✕Wrong BP Grouping — controls the number range for the vendor and cannot be changed after the BP is created.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB60Post the first vendor invoice after the vendor is created
FBL1NView all transactions for this vendor after invoices are posted
FK10NView the vendor period balance summary
FB60

Enter Vendor Invoice

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Enter a vendor invoice from supplier 580024 (المتحدة) for Cleaning Expenses of EGP 8,000. Post the invoice and verify the resulting accounting document.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Posts a vendor invoice into SAP — records the liability (credit to AP reconciliation account) and the corresponding expense or asset (debit to GL). This is the core Accounts Payable data entry transaction.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

You receive an invoice from a cleaning company for 8,000 EGP. Enter it in FB60: Vendor = 580024, Amount = 8,000, GL = 500005. SAP creates the entry: Dr Cleaning Expense 8,000 / Cr AP Reconciliation 8,000.

1
Open FB60 — Enter Vendor Invoice
Type FB60 in the command field. The "Enter Vendor Invoice: Company Code BEV1" screen opens. The header shows:
• Transaction: R Invoice
• Balance indicator: ○△○ (unbalanced — red/yellow until entry is complete)
• Tabs: Basic data | Payment | Details | Tax | Withholding tax | Notes
• Company Code: BEV1 be7ery Madinty (at bottom)
💡 FB60 automatically sets the document type to KR (Vendor Invoice) and posts to the vendor sub-ledger + reconciliation account. No need to enter posting keys manually.
2
Fill Header Data — Vendor, Date, Amount
On the Basic data tab, fill in:

• Vendor: 580024 → press Enter → a vendor address popup appears showing: Company: المتحدة, Cairo, Egypt
• Invoice date: 05/15/2026 (today)
• Posting Date: auto-fills to 05/15/2026
• Amount: 8,000 EGP

The balance indicator changes as you fill the amount. Payment terms show "Due immediately" and Baseline Date auto-fills.
📌 The vendor popup confirms you selected the correct supplier before proceeding. Check the address to avoid posting to the wrong vendor.
💡 Payment Terms field: On the Payment tab (next to Basic data), SAP pre-fills the Payment Terms from the vendor master — e.g. 0001 = Pay immediately. This field controls the due date SAP calculates for the open item in FBL1N. You can override it per invoice if agreed terms differ. The due date = Baseline date + Payment Terms days. Always verify payment terms match the invoice before posting.
3
Enter Line Item — GL Account & Cost Center
In the line items grid at the bottom of the screen, fill in the expense line:

• G/L acct: 500005 → Short Text auto-fills: "Cleanning exp"
• D/C: S Debit (auto-set for expense)
• Amount in doc.curr.: 8,000
• Cost center: INVCS01

The result grid shows: 500005 | Cleanning exp | S Deb | 8,000 | BEV1 | INVCS01
💡 In FB60, you only enter the expense/debit side. SAP automatically creates the vendor credit line (posting to the reconciliation account 210100) behind the scenes.
4
Simulate → Post → Display
Click Simulate in the toolbar to preview the document. The balance indicator should now be green (○○■). The preview shows both lines:

• Item 1 | Key 31 | Vendor 580024 (المتحدة) | 8,000 Cr EGP
• Item 2 | Key 40 | G/L 500005 (Cleanning exp) | 8,000 Dr EGP

If balanced → click Post. SAP creates Document Number 1900000143 (Doc Type: KR). Use FB03 to display and verify.
📌 Posting Key 31 = Vendor Credit (AP). SAP uses this automatically for the vendor line. The document is now in the vendor sub-ledger as an open item (unpaid invoice).
✅ Result: Vendor invoice posted — Doc 1900000143 (KR). Vendor 580024 Cr 8,000 / Cleaning Exp (500005) Dr 8,000 EGP. The invoice is now an open item in FBL1N awaiting payment.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Wrong vendor number — always confirm the vendor name auto-fills correctly; a wrong vendor is difficult to correct after posting.
✕Posting to the wrong GL expense account — the account must match the nature of the invoice for accurate financial reporting.
✕Forgetting payment terms — these determine the invoice due date and directly affect the AP aging report and cash flow planning.
🔗 Related T-Codes
BPThe vendor must be created here first — FB60 will fail without it
FBL1NVerify the invoice appears in vendor line items after posting
FK10NCheck the updated total vendor balance after posting
F-48

Post Vendor Down Payment

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Pay an advance of 5,000 EGP to vendor 580024 in Company Code BEV1 before the invoice arrives, using special G/L indicator A.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Posts an advance payment to a vendor before the invoice is received. Recorded as a special G/L transaction (indicator A) on an alternative reconciliation account, kept separate from normal payables.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

A supplier requires 30% upfront before manufacturing. AP posts the advance with F-48 so it is tracked separately and later netted against the final invoice.

1
Open F-48 & enter the header
Type F-48. Enter Document Date, Posting Date, Company Code BEV1, Currency. In the Vendor section: Account 580024, Special G/L indicator: A. In the Bank section: House Bank / Account ID, Amount 5,000.
Indicator A flags this as a down payment, not a normal payment.
2
Process & post
Press Enter → SAP proposes the down payment line against the alternative (down payment) reconciliation account. Verify the amount → Post.
Posting = Bank Cr 5,000 / Vendor special G/L (down payment) Dr 5,000.
✅ Result: Advance of 5,000 posted to vendor 580024 as special G/L indicator A. Visible in FBL1N under Special G/L transactions; the normal 210100 payables balance is unaffected.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Forgetting the Special G/L indicator A — the advance posts as a normal payment instead of a down payment.
✕Using the wrong house bank or bank account ID.
✕Posting to the standard recon account instead of the configured alternative one (configured in FBKP).
🔗 Related T-Codes
F-47Raise the down payment request first
FBKPConfigure the special G/L (down payment) account
F-54Clear the down payment against the invoice
FBKP

Maintain Accounting Config (Special G/L)

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Configure which alternative G/L account vendor down payments (account type K, indicator A) should post to.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

The configuration cockpit for automatic account determination and special G/L transactions. Used here to link a special G/L indicator (A = down payment) to its alternative reconciliation account.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

During implementation, the consultant sets up special G/L so that every F-48 down payment automatically posts to the 'Advances to Vendors' account instead of normal payables.

1
Open FBKP & choose Special G/L
Type FBKP → select the Special G/L option from the configuration menu.
2
Select account type & indicator
Choose Account type: K (vendor) — or D for customers. Choose the special G/L indicator: A (down payment) or F (down payment request).
K = Kreditor (vendor), D = Debitor (customer).
3
Assign the special G/L account & save
Enter the alternative reconciliation (down payment) account against the normal recon account → Save.
The account must already exist in FS00 and be flagged as a reconciliation account.
✅ Result: Special G/L indicator A is linked to the down payment account. F-48 / F-47 now post automatically to the correct alternative account.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Treating FBKP as a daily transaction — it is configuration, run once during setup.
✕Linking to a G/L account that is not flagged as a reconciliation account.
✕Configuring the wrong account type (K for vendors vs D for customers).
🔗 Related T-Codes
F-48Uses this config to post the down payment
F-47Down payment request (indicator F)
FS00Create the special G/L account first
F-47

Down Payment Request

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Raise a down payment request for vendor 580024 so the payment program / F-48 can later pay the advance.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Creates a down payment request — a noted item (statistical, indicator F) recording the intention to pay an advance. No GL posting and no balance change.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

Procurement agrees a prepayment; AP raises an F-47 request so the next payment run automatically picks it up and pays the vendor.

1
Open F-47 & enter data
Type F-47. Enter the header (date, Company Code BEV1) and Vendor 580024. Add the requested amount. Special G/L indicator: F.
Indicator F = down payment request.
2
Post the noted item
Post → SAP records a noted item. No real accounting document and no change to the vendor balance.
It appears in the vendor account as a trigger for the actual payment.
✅ Result: Down payment request (indicator F) recorded for vendor 580024 — visible as a noted item, ready to be paid via F-48 or the payment program.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Expecting a GL impact — a request is statistical only.
✕Using indicator A instead of F.
✕Forgetting the request is just a trigger, not the actual payment.
🔗 Related T-Codes
F-48Post the actual down payment
FBKPSpecial G/L configuration
F-53Outgoing payment
F-53

Post Outgoing Payment

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Pay an open vendor invoice for 580024 from the bank and clear it (document type KZ).
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Pays an open vendor invoice. You enter the bank details and amount, select the open invoice(s) to settle, and post — clearing the payable.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

After approving an invoice, AP runs F-53 to pay the vendor from the house bank and clears the open item so it no longer appears as outstanding.

1
Open F-53 & enter bank data
Type F-53. Enter Document/Posting Date, Company Code BEV1, Currency. Bank data: bank/clearing G/L account, Amount paid.
This is the cash/bank side of the payment.
2
Select the vendor & open items
Enter Vendor 580024 → Process Open Items. Activate the invoice(s) to pay so the assigned amount equals the payment and the balance is 0.
The 'Not assigned' field must be 0.00 before you can post.
3
Post
Post → Bank Cr / Vendor Dr. The invoice is now cleared (document type KZ).
In FBL1N the item moves from Open to Cleared.
✅ Result: Vendor invoice paid and cleared. Document type KZ created; FBL1N shows the clearing document against the original invoice.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Leaving an unassigned balance not equal to 0 — the post is blocked.
✕Selecting the wrong open items to clear.
✕Using the wrong bank / clearing account.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB60The invoice being paid
FBL1NVerify the invoice is now cleared
F-28The AR equivalent — incoming payment
F-54

Clear Vendor Down Payment

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Net a previously posted down payment for vendor 580024 against the actual invoice.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Clears (transfer-posts) a posted down payment against the vendor invoice once it arrives — moving the advance from the special G/L account back to the normal payable so only the net remains open.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

The advance was paid with F-48; the final invoice is now booked. F-54 nets the advance so AP only pays the remaining balance.

1
Open F-54 & enter the invoice
Type F-54. Enter the header, Company Code BEV1, Vendor 580024. Select the relevant invoice the down payment relates to.
F-54 links the advance to a specific invoice.
2
Select & clear the down payment
Choose the down payment line(s) to transfer → Post.
The special G/L down payment is reversed against the normal recon account; only the net amount remains open.
✅ Result: Down payment cleared against the invoice. The special G/L advance is reduced and the net payable reflects what is still owed.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Clearing before the invoice (FB60) is posted.
✕Selecting the wrong invoice to net against.
✕Confusing F-54 (clear down payment) with F-53 (pay invoice).
🔗 Related T-Codes
F-48The down payment being cleared
FB60The vendor invoice
F-53Pay the remaining balance
FBL1N

Vendor Line Item Display

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Use FBL1N to review all vendor transactions for supplier 580024 in Company Code BEV1. View open items, cleared items, and understand the document types and status indicators. Also explore Special G/L transactions (down payments).
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Displays all individual transactions for a specific vendor — invoices, payments, and credit memos. Filters by open (unpaid), cleared (paid), or all items within a selected date range.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

Before the weekly payment run, the AP team opens FBL1N for each vendor to see all outstanding invoices, verify amounts and due dates, and flag any overdue items requiring urgent payment.

1
Open FBL1N — Vendor Line Item Display
Type FBL1N in the command field. The Vendor Line Item Display screen opens with sections:

• Supplier Selection: Supplier Account, Company Code
• Selection Using Search Help
• Line item selection — Status: Open items / Cleared items / All items
• Type: Normal items, Special G/L transactions, Noted items, Parked items

Enter Supplier Account: 580024 | Company Code: BEV1 | Select Open Items | Open at key date: today → Execute.
📌 You can also leave Supplier Account blank and execute to view ALL vendor accounts — useful for a broad review or audit.
2
Read the Results — Open Items
SAP shows the Vendor Line Item Display results for Supplier 580024 (المتحدة), City: cairo. The list shows all unpaid invoices:

Columns: Status, Assignment, DocumentNo, Type, Doc. Date, S, DD, Amount in Local Crcy, LCurr, Clrng doc., Text

Example results:
• 🔴 | — | 1900000127 | KR | 05/12/2026 | ⚡ | 777− EGP
• 🔴 | — | 1900000128 | KR | 05/12/2026 | ⚡ | 666− EGP

The total shows the sum of all open items for this vendor (e.g. "5 items displayed").
💡 🔴 = Open (unpaid). The ⚡ icon in the DD column = Overdue (past net due date). A 🟢 square = Cleared (paid/settled).
3
Switch to All Items — See Cleared Items Too
Go back to the selection screen. Select All Items → Execute. Now the list shows both open (🔴) and cleared (🟢) items. Cleared items show a value in the Clrng doc. column — the document number that cleared/paid the invoice.

Document Types visible:
• KR — Kreditor Rechnung = Vendor Invoice
• KZ — Vendor Payment
• SA — General GL posting related to vendor
📌 To drill into any item, double-click the document number to open the original document in display mode (same as FB03).
4
Special G/L Transactions — Down Payments
Go back to the selection screen. Under Type, check Special G/L transactions (in addition to Normal items). Select All Items → Execute.

The report now shows an additional column S (Special G/L Indicator). Items with indicator A represent down payments made to the vendor. These are tracked separately from regular invoices because they use a different reconciliation account in GL.

Also visible: the Baseline Date (start of payment terms) and Net Due Date (actual payment due date) on each line item.
💡 Special G/L Indicator A = Down Payment. These are prepayments to a vendor — shown separately to ensure they are later matched against the final invoice.
✅ Result: All vendor line items for supplier 580024 displayed. Open items (unpaid invoices) identified by 🔴 and ⚡ indicators. Cleared items visible in All Items view. Down payments visible via Special G/L filter. Document types KR (Invoice) and KZ (Payment) identified.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Forgetting to set the correct Open at Key Date — leaving it as today may miss invoices that were open at a prior month-end.
✕Missing Special G/L items — down payments and guarantees are filtered separately and will not appear in the standard open items view.
✕Confusing open items (unpaid) with cleared items (paid and matched) — check the status indicator column carefully.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FK10NView the vendor period balance summary instead of individual lines
FB60The transaction that created most of the invoice line items shown
IDCNAPAP aging analysis across all vendors simultaneously
FK10N

Vendor Balance Display

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Use FK10N to review the balance of vendor 580024 in Company Code BEV1 for fiscal year 2026. Analyze the period-by-period balance and drill down into a specific period's transactions.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Displays the total debit, credit, and cumulative balance for a vendor account broken down by accounting period. Allows direct drill-down from any period total to the individual transactions behind it.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

The Finance Manager asks: "How much did we owe Coca-Cola at the end of Q1 2026?" Open FK10N, enter the vendor and fiscal year — the cumulative balance at period 3 answers the question in one screen.

1
Open FK10N — Enter Parameters
Type FK10N in the command field. The Vendor Balance Display screen opens with three fields:

• Vendor: 580024
• Company code: BEV1
• Fiscal year: 2026

Click Execute.
💡 FK10N is the vendor equivalent of FAGLB03 (GL Balance Display). It gives you a month-by-month summary of all activity for this vendor account.
2
Read the Vendor Balance Display
The result screen shows vendor 580024 (المتحدة), Company Code BEV1, Fiscal Year 2026, Currency EGP.

Two tabs: Bals (normal balances) and Special G/L (down payments).

The Bals table columns: Period | Debit | Credit | Balance | Cumulative Balance | Sales/Purchases

Example result for Period 5:
• Debit: 1,887 | Credit: 11,330 | Balance: 9,443− | Cumulative Balance: 9,443− | Sales/Purchases: 11,330−

Periods 6–12 show the Cumulative Balance carried forward (9,443−) even with no new activity.
📌 A negative cumulative balance means the company owes this vendor — the vendor has a credit position (you haven't paid yet).
3
Drill Down — Double-Click on Amount
To see the individual transactions behind any period's total, double-click on the amount in the Credit or Balance column for that period. SAP opens the Vendor Line Item Display (FBL1N) filtered to that period and vendor — showing each individual invoice or payment that makes up that period's total.

Example: double-clicking Period 5 Credit (11,330) shows the invoices posted in May 2026 for vendor 580024.
✅ This drilldown capability is the power of FK10N — quickly navigate from a summary balance to the exact underlying transactions.
✅ Result: Vendor 580024 balance displayed by period. Period 5 shows Debit 1,887 / Credit 11,330 / Balance 9,443−. Cumulative balance of 9,443− EGP indicates this vendor is owed money. Drilldown to individual transactions available via double-click.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Misreading a negative cumulative balance — in AP, a negative balance is normal and simply means the company owes that vendor money.
✕Forgetting to check the Special G/L tab — down payments appear there separately and are excluded from the main Bals tab.
✕Wrong fiscal year — FK10N shows one fiscal year at a time; always verify you selected the correct year.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FBL1NSee every individual invoice and payment behind any period total
IDCNAPAP aging analysis for all vendors simultaneously
FB60The transaction that created the vendor invoices shown in the balance
IDCNAP

AP Aging Report

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Generate an AP Aging Report for Company Code BEV1 as of today's date. View all outstanding vendor invoices grouped by aging buckets (Not Due, <11 days, 11–20, 21–30, 31–60 days, etc.) to identify overdue payables.
1
Open IDCNAP — A/P Aging Report
Type IDCNAP in the command field. The A/P Aging Report selection screen opens with four sections:

• Supplier Selection: Supplier Account (leave blank for all) | Company Code: BEV1 to BEV1
• Transaction Figures Selection: Profit Center, Segment (optional)
• Line Item Selection: Open Items at Key Date → 05/15/2026 (today)
• Further Selections: Normal Items ✓, Special G/L Transactions (optional)
📌 Leave Supplier Account blank to include ALL vendors in the company — this gives the complete AP aging picture for management reporting.
2
Configure Output Control — Due Date Sorted List
Scroll down to the Output Control section. The Due Date Sorted List defines the aging buckets:

Default values: 10 | 20 | 30 | 60 | 90 | 180 | 360 | 720 days (each in 1-Day intervals)

These buckets mean: Not Due | 1–10 days | 11–20 days | 21–30 days | 31–60 days | 61–90 days | etc.

Leave as default → Click Execute.
💡 You can customize the aging buckets to match your company's payment terms. For example, if standard terms are Net 30, use buckets of 30, 60, 90 days.
3
Read the AP Aging Analysis Report
The AP Aging Analysis screen opens showing all vendors with open items as of the key date. Columns:

CoCd | Segment | Profit Center | Supplier | Supplier Name | G/L Acct | G/L Acc Text | Σ Total Open | LCurr | Σ Not Due | % | Σ <11D | % | Σ 11D–20D | % | Σ 21D–30D | % | Σ 31D–60D…

Example results (Company BEV1):
• Cocacola (1): Total Open 2,800 EGP | 100% in <11 Days
• Aroma (10079): Total Open 7,500 EGP | 100% in <11 Days
• Behery (446031): Total Open 600 EGP | 100% Not Due
• المتحدة (580024): Total Open 8,000 EGP | 100% in <11 Days
📌 The % column shows what proportion of the vendor's total payable falls in each aging bucket. 100% in <11 Days means the invoice is recent and not yet overdue.
✅ Result: AP Aging Report generated for Company Code BEV1 as of 05/15/2026. All outstanding vendor invoices grouped by aging period. Report shows total payables by vendor with % distribution across aging buckets — essential for cash flow planning and payment prioritization.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Wrong Key Date — the aging is calculated as of this date; an incorrect date produces completely misleading overdue figures.
✕Not customizing the aging buckets — the default buckets may not match your company's payment terms (e.g. Net 45 or Net 60).
✕Forgetting Special G/L transactions — down payments may need to be included or excluded depending on the analysis purpose.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FBL1NDrill into individual invoice details for any specific vendor
FK10NView a single vendor's balance broken down by period
FB60The transaction that created the outstanding invoices shown in the report
FB70

Enter Customer Invoice

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Bill customer CocaCola in Company Code BEV1 — debit the customer, credit revenue 400000.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Enters a customer (outgoing) invoice in FI — the AR counterpart of FB60. Debits the customer (receivable) and credits a revenue account. Document type DR.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

Sales issues a billing document; AR enters the corresponding customer invoice in FI so the receivable and revenue are recognised in the ledger.

1
Open FB70 & choose the customer
Type FB70 (Company Code BEV1). Enter the Customer (e.g. CocaCola), Invoice date and total Amount.
The header shows the customer's address.
2
Enter the revenue line & details
In the line items: G/L Account = Revenue 400000 with the amount. Add Profit Center and Profitability Segment for margin / segment reporting.
Revenue is credited; the customer is debited automatically.
3
Simulate & post
Simulate → Customer Dr / Revenue Cr → Post. Document type DR.
DR = Debitor Rechnung = customer invoice.
✅ Result: Customer invoice posted (type DR). Receivable on the customer reconciliation account, revenue on 400000. Visible in FBL5N.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Posting to the wrong revenue account.
✕Forgetting the profit center / profitability segment where required.
✕Confusing FB70 (customer) with FB60 (vendor).
🔗 Related T-Codes
FB60The vendor equivalent — vendor invoice
FBL5NDisplay the customer line item
F-28Receive payment for this invoice
FBL5N

Customer Line Item Display

Beginner
📋 Scenario
Review all line items for customer CocaCola in BEV1 — open, cleared, all — plus special G/L (down payments).
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Displays all individual transactions for a customer — invoices, payments, credit memos. Filters by open / cleared / all. The AR counterpart of FBL1N.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

Before chasing a customer for payment, AR opens FBL5N to see exactly which invoices are still open and which are overdue.

1
Open FBL5N & select
Type FBL5N. Enter the Customer Account (or leave blank for all) and Company Code BEV1. Choose Open / Cleared / All Items and the key date → Execute.
2
Read the results
Columns: Status, DocumentNo, Type, Date, Amount, Clearing doc, Text. 🔴 open, ⚡ overdue, 🟢 cleared. Doc type DR = customer invoice, DZ = customer payment.
Double-click any line to open the source document (same as FB03).
3
Special G/L view
Under Type, tick Special G/L transactions → Execute. Indicator A items = customer down payments, shown separately.
Same logic as vendors (FBL1N), customer side.
✅ Result: All customer line items displayed with status; open/overdue receivables identified; down payments visible via the special G/L filter.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕A wrong key date hides items that were open at a prior month-end.
✕Missing special G/L items — down payments are filtered separately.
✕Confusing open (unpaid) with cleared (paid) items.
🔗 Related T-Codes
FBL1NThe vendor equivalent
FD10NCustomer balance summary
IDCNARAR aging across all customers
FD10N

Customer Balance Display

Beginner
📋 Scenario
View customer CocaCola's balance period-by-period for fiscal year 2026.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Shows the customer account balance by period (monthly) — a summary view. The AR counterpart of FK10N.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

At month-end, AR reviews each major customer's running balance to reconcile receivables before reporting.

1
Open FD10N & enter selection
Type FD10N. Enter Customer, Company Code BEV1, Fiscal year 2026 → Execute.
2
Read & drill down
The table shows Period (1–12) with Debit, Credit, Balance, Cumulative Balance. The Bals tab = normal items; the Special G/L tab = down payments. Double-click a period to drill into FBL5N for that period.
FD10N is the customer equivalent of FK10N / FAGLB03.
✅ Result: Customer balance shown period by period; drill-down into individual line items available per period.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕A wrong fiscal year returns no data.
✕Looking only at Bals and missing special G/L down payments.
✕Confusing FD10N (customer) with FK10N (vendor).
🔗 Related T-Codes
FK10NVendor balance display
FBL5NCustomer line items
FAGLB03GL account balance display
IDCNAR

AR Aging Report

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Generate the Accounts Receivable aging report for all customers in BEV1 at today's key date.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Generates the AR aging report — how long customer invoices have been outstanding, grouped into aging buckets. The AR counterpart of IDCNAP.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

The credit / collections team uses the AR aging weekly to prioritise which overdue customers to chase and to assess bad-debt risk.

1
Open IDCNAR & select
Type IDCNAR. Customer selection: blank for all, or a specific Customer / Company Code. Line items: Open items at key date (today).
Shows all unpaid invoices as of that date.
2
Set aging buckets & execute
Output Control → Due Date Sorted List: 10, 20, 30, 60, 90, 180, 360, 720 days → Execute.
This defines the aging columns.
3
Read the analysis
Columns: CoCd, Customer, Name, G/L Account, Total Open, Not Due, <11 Days, 11–20, 21–30, 31–60… Each row is a customer split across aging buckets.
Helps prioritise collections by how overdue each receivable is.
✅ Result: AR aging analysis produced; total receivable per customer split across aging buckets at the chosen key date.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕A wrong key date skews the aging buckets.
✕Forgetting to set the due-date sorted list — no buckets appear.
✕Confusing IDCNAR (customer) with IDCNAP (vendor).
🔗 Related T-Codes
IDCNAPAP aging report (vendors)
FBL5NDrill into a customer's items
FD10NCustomer balance
F-28

Post Incoming Payment

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Receive payment from customer CocaCola and clear the open invoice (document type DZ).
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Posts money received from a customer and clears the matching open invoice(s). The AR counterpart of F-53.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

A customer pays; AR posts the incoming payment to the bank and clears the open invoice so it no longer shows as a receivable.

1
Open F-28 & enter bank data
Type F-28. Header (date, Company Code BEV1, Currency). Bank data: bank/clearing G/L account and Amount received.
This is the bank side of the receipt.
2
Select customer & open items
Enter the Customer → Process Open Items. Activate the invoice(s) so the assigned amount = the receipt and the balance is 0 → Post.
Document type DZ; the invoice moves to Cleared in FBL5N.
✅ Result: Incoming payment posted and the customer invoice cleared (document type DZ).
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Leaving an unassigned balance not equal to 0.
✕Selecting the wrong open items.
✕Using the wrong bank account.
🔗 Related T-Codes
F-53The vendor equivalent — outgoing payment
FB70The invoice being paid
FBL5NVerify the invoice is cleared
F-29

Post Customer Down Payment

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Record an advance received from customer CocaCola as a special G/L transaction (indicator A).
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Posts an advance received from a customer before the invoice — a special G/L transaction. The AR counterpart of F-48.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

A customer pays a deposit before delivery; AR records it with F-29 so it is tracked separately from normal receivables until netted against the invoice.

1
Open F-29 & enter data
Type F-29. Header, Bank data (account, amount received), Customer account, Special G/L indicator: A.
A = down payment received.
2
Post
Press Enter → SAP proposes the down payment line against the alternative recon account → Post.
Posting = Bank Dr / Customer special G/L (advance) Cr.
✅ Result: Customer advance posted as special G/L A; visible in FBL5N under special G/L; normal receivables unaffected.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Forgetting the Special G/L indicator A.
✕Using the wrong special G/L account (configured in FBKP, account type D).
✕Treating it as a normal incoming payment.
🔗 Related T-Codes
F-48The vendor equivalent
F-39Clear the advance against the invoice
FBKPSpecial G/L config for account type D
F-39

Clear Customer Down Payment

Intermediate
📋 Scenario
Net a customer advance against the actual invoice for CocaCola.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

Clears a posted customer down payment against the customer invoice once issued — moving the advance from special G/L back to the normal receivable so only the net remains open. The AR counterpart of F-54.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

The deposit was received via F-29; the invoice is now posted. F-39 nets the advance so only the net receivable remains open.

1
Open F-39 & enter the invoice
Type F-39. Header, Company Code BEV1, Customer. Select the relevant invoice the advance relates to.
Links the advance to a specific invoice.
2
Select & clear
Choose the down payment line(s) → Post.
The special G/L advance is reversed against the normal recon account; only the net remains open.
✅ Result: Customer down payment cleared against the invoice; the net receivable reflects what is still due.
⚠️ Common Mistakes
✕Clearing before the invoice (FB70) exists.
✕Selecting the wrong invoice to net against.
✕Confusing F-39 (clear advance) with F-28 (receive payment).
🔗 Related T-Codes
F-29The advance being cleared
FB70The customer invoice
F-54The vendor equivalent
F110

Automatic Payment Run

Advanced
📋 Scenario
Run the weekly automatic payment run for Company Code BEV1. Pay all due vendor invoices via bank transfer. Walk through every phase: parameters → proposal → exception review → post payment → check the GL and AP line items.
🔍 What is this T-Code?

F110 is SAP's Automatic Payment Program. It scans all open vendor invoices, checks due dates and payment terms, selects items eligible for payment, and posts the payment documents automatically — crediting the bank account and clearing (closing) the open vendor invoices. Used by every company on SAP to run weekly or bi-weekly vendor payment batches.

💼 Real-Life Use Case

Every Thursday, the AP team runs F110 to pay all vendors whose invoices are due in the next 7 days. The run selects 47 invoices across 12 vendors, posts payment documents totalling 320,000 EGP, and sends a payment file to the bank — all in one automated process rather than 47 individual F-53 manual payments.

⚡ The 4 Phases of Every F110 Run
1 Parameters — Define who to pay, from which bank, up to what date, via which payment method.
2 Proposal — SAP scans and selects all eligible invoices. No postings yet — this is a preview. Review before proceeding.
3 Exception List — SAP flags invoices it could NOT pay (blocked, missing payment method, house bank not configured). Must be resolved before posting.
4 Payment Run (Post) — SAP posts all payment documents, clears the vendor invoices, and debits the bank GL account. Irreversible after this point.
1
Open F110 — The Initial Screen
Type F110 in the command field and press Enter. The Automatic Payment Transactions: Status screen opens. Every F110 run is identified by two keys at the top:

• Run Date: Today's date — e.g. 06.06.2026
• Identification: A short text ID for this run — e.g. WEEK01

Enter these two fields first. SAP uses this combination as the unique identifier for the run. You can retrieve any previous run by entering its Run Date and Identification.
📌 The Run Date is not the payment date — it is just the ID key for this execution. The actual payment date is set in the Parameters tab. Always use a meaningful Identification so you can find the run later (e.g. WEEK01, PAYRUN-JUN, etc.).
2
Tab: Parameters — Define What to Pay
Click the Parameters tab. Fill in the required fields:

• Posting Date: 06.06.2026 — the date on the payment documents that will be created
• Docs Entered Up To: 06.06.2026 — only invoices entered on or before this date will be included
• Company Codes: BEV1
• Payment Methods: T (Bank Transfer) — must match the payment method configured in the vendor master
• Next P/Date: 13.06.2026 — the next planned run date; SAP uses this to decide which due invoices to include now vs defer to next run

Scroll down to the Accounts section:
• Vendors (From / To): Leave blank to include all vendors, or enter a range to limit the run to specific vendor accounts.
💡 The "Next Payment Date" is critical. SAP pays invoices whose due date falls between today and the next run date. If an invoice is due on 10.06 and next run is 13.06, SAP includes it now. If it's due on 20.06 and there's another run before that, SAP defers it.
3
Tab: Free Selection — Optional Filters
The Free Selection tab lets you add additional filters — for example, pay only invoices above a certain amount, or exclude a specific document type. For this exercise, leave it empty (no additional restrictions).
📌 Free Selection is useful in production when you need to exclude credit memos or run payments for a specific cost center subset. For standard weekly runs, this tab is typically left blank.
4
Save Parameters → Run Proposal
Click Save (Ctrl+S) to save the parameters. The Status screen updates to show: "Parameters have been entered."

Now click Proposal button (or go to Edit → Proposal → Create Proposal). A dialog asks for Start Date and Start Immediately — check Start Immediately and click the green checkmark. SAP runs a background job to scan all open vendor invoices matching your parameters.

After a few seconds, click Refresh on the Status screen. The status updates to: "Proposal has been created."
⚠️ The Proposal does NOT post anything. It is a completely safe preview step. SAP is only reading data at this stage — no documents are created, no balances change. You can delete and recreate the proposal as many times as needed.
5
